docs.title: ホモロジーと圏論
docs.text: エミー・ネーターは、ベッチ数と捩れ係数を商群として整理し、ベッチ群の概念を導入した。

docs.title: 代数：自由関手と忘却関手
docs.text:
This sentence uses inline math: \( \sqrt{3x - 1} + (1 + x)^2 \)

\(k\) を体とする。忘却関手 \( U \)、自由関手 \( F \) と書くと、

\[ \mathbf{Vect}_k \;\leftrightarrows\; \mathbf{Set} \]

これは典型的な随伴の例である。

docs.title: 関連ノート
docs.text: • Algebra

docs.title: Topological Spaces
docs.text:
Definition

A topological space is a pair \((X, \mathcal{T})\) where \(\mathcal{T} \subseteq \mathcal{P}(X)\) satisfies:

  1. \(\emptyset, X \in \mathcal{T}\)
  2. Arbitrary unions of elements of \(\mathcal{T}\) are in \(\mathcal{T}\)
  3. Finite intersections of elements of \(\mathcal{T}\) are in \(\mathcal{T}\)

docs.title: Open and Closed Sets
docs.text:
• Open set: \(U \in \mathcal{T}\)
  • Interior: $$ \operatorname{int}(A) $$
  • Closure: $$ \overline{A} $$
  • Boundary: $$ \partial A = \overline{A} \setminus \operatorname{int}(A) $$

docs.title: Basis of a Topology
docs.text:
Definition

A basis \(\mathcal{B}\) of a topology \(\mathcal{T}\) is a collection such that every open set is a union of elements of \(\mathcal{B}\).

docs.title: \(T_1\) Space
docs.text: A space where all singleton sets are closed.

docs.title: Hausdorff Space
docs.text:
Definition

A space is Hausdorff if for any \(x \neq y\), there exist disjoint open sets \(U, V\) such that: $$ x \in U, \quad y \in V $$

docs.title: Compactness
docs.text:
Definition

A space is compact if every open cover has a finite subcover.

docs.title: Connectedness
docs.text: A space is connected if it cannot be written as a disjoint union of two non-empty open sets.

docs.title: Magma
docs.text:
Definition

A magma is a set \(M\) with a binary operation: $$ M \times M \to M $$

docs.title: Semigroup
docs.text: A magma with associativity: $$ (a b)c = a(b c) $$

docs.title: Monoid
docs.text: A semigroup with identity element \(e\): $$ a e = e a = a $$

docs.title: Group
docs.text:
Definition

A group is a monoid where every element has an inverse: $$ \forall a \in G, \exists a^{-1} \in G $$

docs.title: Abelian Group
docs.text: A group satisfying: $$ a b = b a $$

docs.title: Ring
docs.text:
Definition

A ring is a set \(R\) with two operations \((+, \cdot)\) such that:

  • \((R, +)\) is an abelian group
  • Multiplication is associative

docs.title: Polynomial Rings
docs.text: A ring of the form: $$ R[x] $$

docs.title: Ideals
docs.text:
Definition

An ideal \(I \subseteq R\) satisfies: $$ rI \subseteq I $$

docs.title: Noetherian Rings
docs.text: A ring satisfying the ascending chain condition on ideals.

docs.title: Field
docs.text:
Definition

A field is a commutative ring where every nonzero element is invertible.

docs.title: Field Extensions
docs.text: A field extension: $$ K \subseteq L $$

docs.title: Galois Theory
docs.text: Studies the correspondence: $$ \text{Field Extensions} \leftrightarrow \text{Groups} $$

docs.title: Cohomology
docs.text: Used to study algebraic structures via derived functors.

docs.title: 開集合、開核、閉包、内点、触点、外点、境界点、集積点、孤立点、近傍
コンパクト性とハウスドルフ空間
